name: l2_served_two_pass
description: For reductions with moderate dimension (32-128), a two-pass approach (pass1: compute stats, pass2: normalize) is FASTER than single-pass despite 2x logical reads. The register savings from not holding all values enable higher occupancy, and L2 cache serves the re-reads at near-zero HBM cost.
when_to_apply: When reduction dimension is 32-128 elements. Single-pass requires holding all values in registers (kills occupancy). Two-pass uses L2 for re-reads. NOT useful when reduction dim is very large (L2 can't hold the data) or very small (registers are fine).

evidence.detail: L2 hit rate ~100% on pass 2 for C=64
